Source_Species.reason: Described as a native yeast ECR encoded by TSC13; yeast is Saccharomyces cerevisiae in this study.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The host organism throughout is yeast Saccharomyces cerevisiae; TSC13 is explicitly a native yeast gene.

Applied_Species.reason: TSC13 manipulations were performed in yeast strains.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The applied species was Saccharomyces cerevisiae.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: All yeast strains are derived from CEN.PK2-1D, referred to as CEN.PK2.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Results state native Tsc13p may reduce p-coumaroyl-CoA to p-dihydrocoumaroyl-CoA, leading SlCHS to produce dihydro-coumaroyl anthranilate amide (2).

Core_Validation_Method.reason: They partially disrupted TSC13, replaced it with GhECR2 or MdECR, and analyzed products by LC-MS/MS/MRM with standards validating compounds (2) and (3).

Other_Important_Info.reason: Paper states TSC13 is a native yeast enzyme, not part of tomato cluster, and full deletion inhibited growth because of its essential role in fatty acid synthesis.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: TSC13 is explicitly described as a native yeast ECR/enoyl-CoA reductase.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: No subcellular localization for TSC13 is stated in this paper.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: Deletion of TSC13 inhibited cellular growth due to its essential role in fatty acid synthesis.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All three summarized findings match the Results: native TSC13 caused reduced derivative (2); partial disruption lowered (2); heterologous replacements abolished (2) and revealed (3).

Interacting_Proteins.reason: GhECR2 and MdECR are explicit replacements of TSC13, but direct protein-protein interactions with SlCHS/SlMT2/Sl4CL are not shown; only pathway-level functional interplay is supported.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: Directly inferable from the Results: eliminating/replacing TSC13 avoids reductive side product formation during yeast pathway reconstruction.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: TSC13 is a native yeast gene and was not specifically expression-profiled in the paper.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: The affected products (2) and (3) are hydroxycinnamic acid amides.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Yeast culture media/supernatant was analyzed for these products.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: TSC13 knockout/replacement strains were cultured with 100 μM p-coumaric acid and 100 μM 3-HAA methyl ester.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: The study did not test biological function of the metabolites; this statement matches the paper.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: TSC13 affects whether the pathway yields dihydro-coumaroyl anthranilate amide (2) or coumaroyl anthranilate amide (3).

corrections.reason: No subcellular localization for TSC13 is stated in this paper.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Tomato is given as Solanum lycopersicum.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The applied species was Saccharomyces cerevisiae.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: Supported by individual gene expression strains, feeding assays, and LC-MS/MS/qToF-MS analyses.

Other_Important_Info.reason: Paper states SlMT1 and SlMT2 activities appear redundant for producing (1) and (2), but SlMT1 yields much lower levels so focus shifted to SlMT2.

Applied_Promoters.reason: These vector/promoter-terminator sets are listed in Methods for creating pathway strains, but the paper does not map a specific promoter to SlMT1 in the Results.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: SlMT1/2/3 are discussed in context of the SABATH methyltransferase family.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: No subcellular localization for SlMT1 is reported.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: Lower productivity than SlMT2 is explicitly shown and discussed.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All three points match Results and Fig. 1/2.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: SlMT1 functionally works in strains with SlCHS and Sl4CL, but no direct protein interactions are demonstrated.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: Reasonable direct inference from lower but functional pathway support in yeast.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: It was heterologously expressed in engineered yeast.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: The pathway products (2)/(3) are hydroxycinnamic acid amides.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Products were measured in yeast culture media.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: They tested substrate feeding including 3-HAA and p-coumaric acid in yeast.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: No specific function of the product was tested.

Omics_Data.reason: Untargeted metabolomics by qToF-MS was used, and no public accession is provided in the paper.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: For SlMT1, the paper supports production of (1) and the reduced product (2) in the intact yeast background, not direct support for coumaroyl anthranilate amide (3) as its terminal product.
